user_query:
I'm a neurologist prototyping a lightweight, repeatable "evidence digest" builder for common cognitive interventions my clinic discusses with patients. Please create a small, local prototype that does the following using the provided inputs:

1) For each row in input/interventions.csv, use a search engine to find exactly one official evidence summary or overview page from one of the organizations listed in input/allowed_sources.csv (match by org_name and domain_hint). Do not use commercial blogs or news sites. Prefer an HTML page (not a PDF) so we can extract text.

2) Save a local HTML snapshot of the chosen page to data/raw/<intervention_id>_<shortslug>.html (e.g., data/raw/1_aerobic_mci.html). Also record the source URL you selected.

3) Build data/metadata/sources.jsonl where each line is a JSON object with at least these fields per intervention_id:
   - intervention_id (int)
   - intervention_label (string, from the input file)
   - chosen_source_org (must exactly match one org_name from input/allowed_sources.csv)
   - source_url (string)
   - source_domain (hostname only, e.g., "nih.gov")
   - page_title (string, from the HTML <title> if available)
   - retrieved_on (ISO date, YYYY-MM-DD)
   - snapshot_path (relative path to the saved HTML file)

4) Create data/digest/interventions_summary.csv with one row per intervention_id containing:
   - intervention_id
   - chosen_source_org
   - page_title
   - retrieved_on
   - source_domain
   - local_word_count (number of whitespace-delimited words in the visible text extracted from the saved HTML)
   - contains_year (true/false if any 4-digit year between 1990 and the current year appears in the page text)

5) Implement a minimal validator at tests/validate.py (pure Python 3, no external packages) that:
   - Loads input/interventions.csv and input/allowed_sources.csv.
   - Confirms data/metadata/sources.jsonl has exactly one entry per intervention_id in the input.
   - Verifies each source_domain ends with one of the domain hints listed for the chosen_source_org (supporting multi-option hints separated by "|").
   - Checks that each snapshot_path exists and is non-empty.
   - Confirms data/digest/interventions_summary.csv exists and has the same number of rows as interventions.csv.
   - Writes tests/validation_report.json summarizing pass/fail and per-intervention checks, and prints "VALIDATION PASSED" on success (non-zero exit on failure).

6) Run the validator and save the console output to tests/validate_run.txt.

7) Compose a concise status report at report/status.md with:
   - A brief description of your search queries used per intervention_id and the rationale for the chosen source.
   - A file inventory listing the created files under data/ and tests/ with their sizes in bytes.
   - Any parsing limitations (e.g., if you had to switch sources to get HTML) and proposed next steps for the prototype.

Key constraints:
- Do not hardcode any URLs in advance; identify pages by searching and selecting from the allowed organizations only (see input/allowed_sources.csv). Avoid linking to paywalled content.
- Use only one official page per intervention_id. If the best available is a PDF, you may use it, but ensure you still fill all JSONL fields; set local_word_count to 0 if you cannot reliably extract words, and explain in the report.
- Keep paths exactly as specified above so the validator can locate files.

Deliverables to create under the workspace:
- data/raw/*.html (one per intervention)
- data/metadata/sources.jsonl
- data/digest/interventions_summary.csv
- tests/validate.py, tests/validation_report.json, tests/validate_run.txt
- report/status.md

Use the inputs as the source of truth for targets and allowed organizations. Make sure the Internet search is actually performed to locate and choose the sources, and ensure the validator passes on the final state. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
